data.title: Magnificent 7 Wipes Out 2026 Gains as Tech Concerns Heighten
data.description:
Every member of the Magnificent 7, which makes up roughly one-third of the S&P 500 (SPX), has posted a negative return for 2026 as of Friday's close.

The disappointing performance comes amid fears of overspending in the AI industry, as well as the effects of the technology on future sales and margins. Microsoft (MSFT) has been hit the hardest with a loss of 17%, while chip leader Nvidia (NVDA) has been the least affected within the group with a loss of about 2%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Tesla Adds Grok to Its Vehicles in Nine European Countries
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) is bringing xAI's Grok to its vehicles across Europe, starting with nine countries. According to Tesla's Europe, Middle East & Africa account on X, the first phase includes the United Kingdom, Ireland, Germany, Switzerland, Austria, Italy, France, Portugal, and Spain, with more markets expected to be added later. Interestingly, Grok will allow drivers to ask questions using real-time information and interact with the system without taking their hands off the wheel.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, the feature includes selectable personalities, such as "Storyteller" and "Unhinged," that will give drivers different response styles. To activate Grok, users can either open it through the App Launcher or press and hold the microphone button on the steering wheel. However, Grok is only available on Model S, Model 3, Model X, Model Y, and Cybertruck vehicles that have an AMD (AMD) infotainment processor.

Vehicles must also be running software version 2025.26 or newer, and navigation command support requires version 2025.44.25 or later. Moreover, Tesla noted that Grok does not replace standard voice commands for vehicle controls like climate settings or media changes. The company also said that Grok interactions are processed securely by xAI and are not connected to individual drivers or specific vehicles. For now, users do not need a separate Grok account or a subscription to access the feature.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Dollar Sentiment at Weakest Level Since 2012, Shows BofA Survey
data.description:
The U.S. Dollar Index, which measures the dollar's value against six major currencies, has fallen by 1.2% year-to-date. More downside could be on the way, according to a Bank of America survey of fund managers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The survey showed that dollar positioning was the most negative since 2012, the year when the bank started to collect data for the statistic.

Weaker Dollar Could Benefit U.S. Growth and Corporate Profits

The falling sentiment comes alongside concerns over the Fed's independence and heightened geopolitical tensions, which could shake confidence in the U.S. economy and reduce foreign demand for the dollar.

"We still see an environment where the dollar can weaken from here," said JPMorgan Asset Management's Iain Stealey.

At the same time, a weaker dollar has benefits as well, such as boosting U.S. exports, increasing earnings for multinational businesses, and lifting gross domestic product (GDP) growth.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Chess Champion Sitting on Huge 35% Loss from Robinhood Stock
data.description:
U.S. Chess champion Hikaru Nakamura is sitting on a huge loss from one of the stocks in his TipRanks investment portfolio.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On September 16 last year, Hikaru bought 55 Robinhood (HOOD) shares at a purchase price of $117.34. Its current value is $4,178.35 and it represents 4.08% of his overall holdings.

Never Ending Story

At the time Hikaru said HOOD had: "Never ending growth, more markets with crypto and "predictions." Incredible app which all the cool kids are using these days. Still has a market cap 40-50B less than Schwab despite more adoption amongst the future generations."

His faith has yet to be rewarded given that he is sitting on a 35.26% loss on HOOD stock. The company's share price is down 32.83% in the year-to-date hit by a drop-off in crypto trading as the price of Bitcoin plunges.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: IBM Plans to Triple Entry-Level Hiring, Even for AI-Exposed Roles
data.description:
Tech giant IBM (IBM) is taking a different approach to artificial intelligence than many of its peers. Instead of reducing hiring for roles that AI is expected to automate, the company plans to triple entry-level hiring in the U.S. in 2026. Speaking at the Leading with AI Summit in New York, Chief Human Resources Officer Nickle LaMoreaux said that this increase will apply even to jobs that many believe AI can now handle. In other words, IBM does not see automation as a reason to shrink its junior-level workforce, as it believes that human employees still play an essential role alongside AI tools.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, IBM has restructured those entry-level roles. Because AI can now complete many routine coding tasks, junior software developers are spending less time writing basic code and more time working directly with customers. Similarly, entry-level HR employees step in when AI chatbots are unable to fully resolve employee questions by helping correct outputs and communicating with managers.

Importantly, LaMoreaux explained that companies that stop hiring early-career workers are at risk of creating a long-term talent gap, as they will not have enough experienced employees in the future. As a result, IBM is focusing on redesigning roles rather than eliminating them, at a time when many tech leaders are warning that AI could significantly reduce entry-level opportunities in the coming years. However, LaMoreaux pushed back on that view by arguing that IBM's strategy is to combine human skills with AI capabilities rather than replace workers entirely.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 13 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $337.53 per share implies 28.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nuclear Stocks Ready to Glow as Small Reactor Takes Cargo Ride Over California
data.description:
Nuclear stocks could be all a glow tomorrow when U.S. markets reopen after a small reactor took a ride on a cargo plane over California at the weekend.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Quick Deployment Power

The U.S. Department of Energy and Defense said that a small nuclear reactor had been transported for the first time ever from California to Utah, demonstrating the mobility of the technology and the potential to quickly deploy power for military and civilian use.

The agencies partnered with California-based Valar Atomics to fly one of the company's Ward microreactors on a C-17 aircraft — without nuclear fuel — to Hill Air Force Base in Utah. Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment Michael Duffey were on the C-17 flight with the reactor and its components.

"This gets us closer to deploy nuclear power when and where it is needed to give our nation's warfighters the tools to win in battle," Duffey said.

It's not just for use in conflict. President Trump sees small nuclear reactors as being a crucial way of boosting domestic U.S. energy production, rather than his hated 'windmills' and renewable energy.

Trump last May issued four executive orders aimed at boosting domestic nuclear deployment to meet growing demand for energy for national security and the growth of energy hungry AI data centers.

In December, the Energy Department issued two new grants to speed up development.

Much Needed Boost

The Energy Department plans to have three microreactors reach "criticality" — when a nuclear reaction can sustain itself — by July 4.

The microreactor on the plane, just bigger than a minivan, is capable of generating up to 5 megawatts of electricity, enough to power 5,000 homes. It will start operating in July at 100 kilowatts and peak at 250 kilowatts this year before ramping up to full capacity.

Valar hopes to start selling power on a test basis in 2027 and become fully commercial in 2028.

The cargo ride could give a lift to NuScale Power (SMR), whose stock price dropped around 21% in 2025 given concerns over commercial agreements.

Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE), which is developing ZEUS, a solid-core battery reactor, and ODIN, a low-pressure coolant reactor, is down 21.30% over the last 12 months. It has been hit by mounting operating losses and a critical bottleneck in the nuclear fuel supply chain that could influence the pace of future deployments.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: M&A News: Hapag-Lloyd to Acquire ZIM Integrated in Major Shipping Industry Deal
data.description:
Hapag-Lloyd (HLAGF) is set to acquire ZIM Integrated Shipping Services (ZIM) in an all-cash transaction valued at about $4.2 billion. The deal unites two long-standing carriers at a time when the industry is dealing with volatile freight rates, shifting trade routes, and rising geopolitical pressures.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hapag-Lloyd will pay $35.00 per share in cash, a 58% premium over ZIM's closing price on February 13, 2026. Importantly, the deal will be funded by Hapag-Lloyd's cash reserves and $2.5 billion in external financing.

The acquisition secures Hapag-Lloyd's status as the world's fifth-largest container shipping company, with a combined fleet exceeding 400 vessels and a total capacity of over 3 million TEU.

The transaction is expected to close by late 2026, pending approval from ZIM shareholders and Israeli regulatory authorities.

Strategic Structure and "New ZIM"

ZIM is viewed as a strategic asset for Israel, which is why the state holds a "golden share" that gives it authority over certain key decisions, including ownership.

To comply with the regulations, the deal includes a partnership with the Israeli private equity firm FIMI Opportunity Funds. As per the agreement, FIMI will establish a new Israeli-flagged entity called "New ZIM." This company will own 16 vessels and focus on strategic trade routes to and from Israel.

Also, "New ZIM" will use the ZIM trademark and receive commercial support from Hapag-Lloyd, including access to a network.

What the Deal Means for Hapag-Lloyd and ZIM

For Hapag-Lloyd, the deal strengthens its position as one of the world's largest container lines. ZIM's fleet, customer base, and strong presence on transpacific and intra-Asia routes give Hapag‑Lloyd more scale and flexibility at a time when shipping companies are rethinking capacity and network planning.

For ZIM, the acquisition provides stability after a difficult period marked by big swings in freight rates and profitability. The company saw record earnings during the pandemic-era shipping boom but later faced margin pressure as rates fell and operating costs rose.

Further, by joining a larger global carrier, ZIM will gain access to wider networks, stronger buying power, and more predictable long-term planning.

Is Hapag-Lloyd a Good Investment?

Turning to Wall Street, HLAGF stock has a Moderate Sell consensus rating based on two Holds and five Sells assigned in the last three months. At $114.76, the average Hapag-Lloyd stock price target implies a 19.71% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Builds $1.5 Billion Inventory of Weight-Loss Pill
data.description:
Eli Lilly (LLY) has built $1.5 billion worth of pre-launch inventory of Orforglipron, the company's new weight-loss pill.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The inventory build-up comes as Eli Lilly anticipates the U.S. Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) approval of the new weight-loss pill this April. Global demand for the weight-loss pill is expected to be huge and Eli Lilly is preparing for a big launch.

Executives at Eli Lilly have said that they plan a large-scale marketing campaign to coincide with shipments of the weight-loss pill this summer. Currently, Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill Zepbound is taken via injection. The pill version of the medication is expected to be more palatable to consumers, especially those who are afraid of needles.

Chasing Rival Novo Nordisk

The big push behind the Orforglipron weight-loss pill also comes as Eli Lilly tries to catch European rival Novo Nordisk (NVO), whose Wegovy weight-loss pill received FDA approval late last year and went on sale this January.

Novo Nordisk has reported brisk initial sales of its Wegovy pill and is pulling out all the stops to gain market share ahead of Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill hitting the market this summer. Eli Lilly's initial stockpile of inventory should help it get out of the gate quickly once it gets the expected FDA clearance.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Citigroup (C) Tells Investors to Expect a 'More Volatile Bull Market'
data.description:
Analysts at Citigroup (C) are telling investors to expect a "more volatile bull market" in 2026 as the disruptions from artificial intelligence (AI) grow more intense.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The bank says that the long-term risks of AI disrupting established business models are rattling global financial markets, leading to increased volatility that is likely to continue over the near term. As such, Citigroup says that investors should expect a "more volatile bull" this year.

AI-related disruption fears are shifting investor focus away from near-term earnings strength and toward longer-term valuation risks, adds the Wall Street lender. Markets are now reacting to doubts about how sustainable profits and growth will be in a more competitive, AI-driven world.

Margin Uncertainty

Citigroup stresses in a note to clients that AI is unlikely to immediately hit earnings, which remain solid. Rather, AI is more likely to raise uncertainty about future margin strength at U.S. companies. If AI-driven productivity gains lead to price competition or require heavy upfront investments, companies may struggle to expand their margins, says the bank.

Citigroup concludes that the current environment reflects case-by-case repositioning rather than wholesale risk reduction. Areas such as software, insurers, financial services, and transportation have come under scrutiny as investors debate which business models are most exposed to disruption from AI.

Is C Stock a Buy?

Citigroup's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average C price target of $131 implies 18.17% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
